source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About us

Garage powers the exchange of critical assets across America’s essential industries. We started with fire departments, where purchasing mission-critical vehicles and equipment used to take months of paperwork, phone calls, and delays. Our agentic platform automates the entire transaction: discovery, payments, freight, paperwork, warranty, and financing. What once took months now takes minutes.

In under a year, we’ve expanded to cities and towns in all 50 states and grown revenue more than 10x. Based in New York, we’ve raised $18M from Y Combinator, Initialized Capital, Infinity Ventures, FJ Labs, and other leading investors. We’re moving fast, growing quickly, and just getting started.

 

About the role

You’ll join a small team working directly with the founders on a platform with real-world impact. We obsess over user experience and ship products end-to-end. You’ll have the opportunity to make a direct, meaningful impact, shaping product strategy and building systems that move essential equipment and vehicles to communities across 50 states.

 - Build and own core systems across our marketplace and operations stack.

 - Automate complex workflows spanning payments, logistics, compliance, and financing.

 - Design APIs, data models, and services that move real assets at scale.

 - Work closely with product and operations to ship quickly and reliably.

 

What we’re looking for

 - You think in systems, not tickets.

 - You get excited by operating in a high-performance startup environment.

 - You would love to be customer-facing.

 - You play for the front of the jersey, having strong opinions, a willingness to question the status quo, and a drive to become a real expert in whatever space you work in. All while being a great team communicator.



Required

 - Strong portfolio or GitHub showcasing full-stack expertise.

 - Proficiency in React, TypeScript, Node.js, and modern frontend and backend frameworks.

 - Experience designing and working with scalable databases and APIs.

 

Nice to have

 - Startup experience as a technical co-founder or early engineer at a Series A/B/C startup.

 - 4+ years of experience.

 

Our values

 - Growth oriented. We invest in people who grow as the company grows. We all should be constantly learning.

 - Humility. Everyone has a lot to give and a lot to learn. We believe in creating an environment where the best ideas win and acknowledging when we are wrong.

 - Customer focused. We have a genuine love for our customers. Customer success is our success.

 - Hack to success. We move fast and take big swings. We always aim for a solution that addresses 80% of the problem in 20% of the time. We make informed bets, launch quickly, and iterate.

 

Why Garage?

 - Our product is something people use everyday to buy the equipment local communities everywhere depend on.

 - We’ve grown revenue over 10x in the last year and are well capitalized with $18M in funding raised total.

 - We have a talent-dense, high-performing team from Ramp, Twitter, Goldman Sachs, Bilt, Wise, Unify, Honey, PayPal, and more.

 - You’ll be one of our first 35 employees, operating as a foundational member of the team, partnering alongside the company’s founders.

 

What we offer

 - Competitive salary and stock options

 - Health, dental and vision insurance (100% covered)

 - Unlimited paid time off every year (in addition to company observed holidays)

 - Daily lunch and dinner

 - $100 monthly wellness stipend

 - 401(k) with employer match

 - Pre-tax commuter benefits

 - Bi-annual team offsite

 - Relocation assistance

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Garage powers the exchange of critical assets across America’s essential industries. We started with fire departments, where purchasing mission-critical vehicles and equipment used to take months of paperwork, phone calls, and delays. Our agentic platform automates the entire transaction: discovery, payments, freight, paperwork, warranty, and financing. What once took months now takes minutes.

In under a year, we’ve expanded to cities and towns in all 50 states and grown revenue more than 10x. Based in New York, we’ve raised $18M from Y Combinator, Initialized Capital, Infinity Ventures, FJ Labs, and other leading investors. We’re moving fast, growing quickly, and just getting started.

About the Go-to-Market team

Garage’s Go-to-Market team is on a mission to build a broader more powerful marketplace by expanding categories and accelerating seller and buyer growth. Our focus on impact and scale has helped drive 10× revenue growth over the last year and we're just getting started.

About the role

 - Own the full sales cycle from sourcing and discovery to closing high-impact deals

 - Identify and close new logos that fuel our marketplace growth

 - Engage directly with customers in person to understand their needs and deliver impact

 - Understand customer challenges and uncover opportunities to solve them

 - Partner with our sales leader to streamline processes and drive operational efficiency

What we're looking for

 - You're competitive and love to win

 - You move fast, have an ownership mentality, and an entrepreneurial mindset

 - You're a relationship builder who deeply enjoys getting to know customers and being the conduit between the business and users

 - You're resilient and tenacious -- always pushing to uncover and win new business opportunities

 - You have strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to articulate value propositions in an effective manner

 - You enjoy storytelling and creating compelling narratives that clearly communicate our value

 - You're excited to champion a product that creates real-world impact for both users and the communities they serve

Required

 - 3+ years of experience in a fast-paced closing-focused role with a proven ability to work quickly and iteratively

 - Proven ability to generate net new business by identifying, pursuing, and closing high-impact opportunities

 - Track record of consistently exceeding sales quotas and driving measurable growth

Nice to have

 - Previous success driving revenue at a venture-backed startup

Our values

 - Growth oriented. We invest in people who grow as the company grows. We all should be constantly learning.

 - Humility. Everyone has a lot to give and a lot to learn. We believe in creating an environment where the best ideas win and acknowledging when we are wrong.

 - Customer focused. We have a genuine love for our customers. Customer success is our success.

 - Hack to success. We move fast and take big swings. We always aim for a solution that addresses 80% of the problem in 20% of the time. We make informed bets, launch quickly, and iterate.

Why Garage?

 - Our product is something people use everyday to buy the equipment local communities everywhere depend on.

 - We’ve grown revenue over 10x in the last year and are well capitalized with $18M in funding raised total.

 - We have a talent-dense, high-performing team from Ramp, Twitter, Goldman Sachs, Bilt, Wise, Unify, Honey, PayPal, and more.

 - You’ll be one of our first 35 employees, operating as a foundational member of the team, partnering alongside the company’s founders.

What we offer

 - Competitive salary and stock options

 - Health, dental and vision insurance (100% covered)

 - Unlimited paid time off every year (in addition to company observed holidays)

 - $100 monthly wellness stipend

 - 401(k) with employer match

 - Bi-annual team offsite

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Growth
source_jobs.description:
About us

Garage powers the exchange of critical assets across America’s essential industries. We started with fire departments, where purchasing mission-critical vehicles and equipment used to take months of paperwork, phone calls, and delays. Our agentic platform automates the entire transaction: discovery, payments, freight, paperwork, warranty, and financing. What once took months now takes minutes.

In under a year, we’ve expanded to cities and towns in all 50 states and grown revenue more than 10x. Based in New York, we’ve raised $18M from Y Combinator, Initialized Capital, Infinity Ventures, FJ Labs, and other leading investors. We’re moving fast, growing quickly, and just getting started.

 

About the role

Growth engineering at Garage builds the systems that turn our sales motion into a machine. The industry runs on PDFs, phone calls, and software built in 2003, and we'd rather build the modern version in-house than wire together someone else's tools.

You'll work closely with our sales team and the dealers, manufacturers, fleet operators, and government buyers who move specialty assets across the country. This involves owning the data infrastructure, internal tools, and AI-powered workflows that fill our pipeline.

 - Build the data infrastructure that finds every prospect in the industry by turning fragmented data into pipelines.

 - Ship internal tools that make our sales team dramatically more effective. This includes outbound automation, lead routing, sequencing, deal-room tooling.

 - Apply AI and LLMs to unstructured data and turn it into signals that fill our pipeline.

 - Build agents that work in the background to compound our sales advantage.

 

What we’re looking for

 - You think in systems, not tickets.

 - You get excited by operating in a high-performance startup environment.

 - You would love to be customer-facing.

 - You play for the front of the jersey, having strong opinions, a willingness to question the status quo, and a drive to become a real expert in whatever space you work in. All while being a great team communicator.

 

Required

 - Extremely AI-native, living on the cutting edge. AI is how you ship.

 - A track record of shipping high-quality products at scale, including with and for agents.

 - Experience designing and working with scalable databases and APIs.

 

Nice to have

 - Startup experience as a technical co-founder or early engineer at a Series A/B/C startup.

 - Experience working with messy real-world data from analog sources.

 - Familiarity with sales and marketing automation tools (Clay, HubSpot, Salesforce, Apollo).

 - 2+ years of experience.

 

Our values

 - Growth oriented. We invest in people who grow as the company grows. We all should be constantly learning.

 - Humility. Everyone has a lot to give and a lot to learn. We believe in creating an environment where the best ideas win and acknowledging when we are wrong.

 - Customer focused. We have a genuine love for our customers. Customer success is our success.

 - Hack to success. We move fast and take big swings. We always aim for a solution that addresses 80% of the problem in 20% of the time. We make informed bets, launch quickly, and iterate.

 

Why Garage?

 - Our product is something people use everyday to buy the equipment local communities everywhere depend on.

 - We’ve grown revenue over 10x in the last year and are well capitalized with $18M in funding raised total.

 - We have a talent-dense, high-performing team from Ramp, Twitter, Goldman Sachs, Bilt, Wise, Unify, Honey, PayPal, and more.

 - You’ll be one of our first 35 employees, operating as a foundational member of the team, partnering alongside the company’s founders.

 

What we offer

 - Competitive salary and stock options

 - Health, dental and vision insurance (100% covered)

 - Unlimited paid time off every year (in addition to company observed holidays)

 - Daily lunch and dinner

 - $100 monthly wellness stipend

 - 401(k) with em

source_jobs.title: Growth Operations Associate
source_jobs.description:
About us

Garage is building the platform that powers commerce across America's essential industries. We started by serving fire departments, where buying mission-critical vehicles and equipment used to take months of paperwork, phone calls, and waiting. We automate the entire transaction: payments, freight, paperwork, warranty, and financing. Months become minutes.

In under a year, we now serve local governments across all 50 states and have grown revenue >10x. We recently closed a $13.5M Series A ($18M total raised) from Infinity Ventures, Y Combinator, Initialized Capital, FJ Labs, and other top investors. We're early, we're moving fast, and we're just getting started.

About the role

In Growth Operations, you are the driving force behind Garage - you will fuel the growth of Garage by driving operational excellence in existing products and launching new markets to increase our reach and ensure the success of our current and future clients. You will work cross-functionally with growth, operations, product, engineering, and leadership in this role. You’ll wear multiple hats and work closely with our customers to achieve their objectives. You will learn what it’s like to be on the ground floor of building and growing a startup.

In this role, you will:

 - Strategize and implement initiatives to streamline operations and growth

 - Become a trusted subject matter expert in all aspects of Garage’s products, and the ways in which we can fulfill our mission of saving customers time and money

 - Analyze market trends, customer behavior, and competitive landscape to identify growth opportunities

 - Tackle a wide range of operational tasks to ‘keep the planes flying’

 - Act as an advocate for Garage’s brand, representing our company’s vision and values to current and prospective partners

 - Build and maintain strong relationships with existing clients, understanding their needs and providing exceptional customer service

We're looking for someone who:

 - Loves the energy of a high-growth environment where every day looks a little different

 - You have good judgement, high integrity, and a no-task-too-small mindset

Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree from an accredited university

 - 2+ years of experience in investment banking, consulting, corporate strategy, ex-Founders, working at a startup, business development, or related roles

 - Analytical mindset with ability to interpret data, detect insights, and implement solutions quickly

 - Exceptional organization skills with the ability to juggle multiple priorities and deadlines

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to effectively engage with customers and internal stakeholders

 - Self-starter who thrives in ambiguous environments, and proactively takes on tasks

 - Meticulous attention to detail, ensuring nothing falls through the cracks




YOU COULD WORK ANYWHERE, SO WHY GARAGE?

 - We’ve grown revenue over 10x in the last year and are well capitalized after a recent $13.5M Series A https://techcrunch.com/2025/08/20/yc-backed-garage-raises-13-5m-to-help-firefighters-buy-equipment/ ($18M in funding raised total).

 - Our product is something people use everyday to buy the equipment local communities everywhere depend on.

 - We have a talent-dense, high-performance team where everyone joining now has an outsized impact.

 - What we’ve built works. We’re backed by world-class vendors: Y Combinator, Infinity Ventures, Initialized Capital, FJ Labs, and more.

 - You’ll be one of our first 35 employees, operating as a foundational member of the team, partnering alongside the company’s founders.

The values that fuel us at Garage:

 - Growth oriented. We invest in people who grow as the company grows. We all should be constantly learning.

 - Humility. Everyone has a lot to give and a lot to learn. We believe in creating an environment where the best ideas win and acknowledging when we are wrong.

 - Customer focused. We have a genuine love for our customers.

source_jobs.title: Strategic Finance Lead
source_jobs.description:
About us

Garage powers the exchange of critical assets across America’s essential industries. We started with fire departments, where purchasing mission-critical vehicles and equipment used to take months of paperwork, phone calls, and delays. Our agentic platform automates the entire transaction: discovery, payments, freight, paperwork, warranty, and financing. What once took months now takes minutes.

In under a year, we’ve expanded to cities and towns in all 50 states and grown revenue more than 10x. Based in New York, we’ve raised $18M from Y Combinator, Initialized Capital, Infinity Ventures, FJ Labs, and other leading investors. We’re moving fast, growing quickly, and just getting started.

 

About the role

As Strategic Finance Lead, you will build the financial operating system that helps Garage scale a transaction-heavy marketplace with speed, discipline, and clarity. Reporting directly to the CEO, you’ll partner with the founders and functional leaders on the company’s most important decisions: where to invest, how to grow efficiently, how to measure marketplace health, and how to allocate capital as the business expands.

This is a hands-on finance role for someone who wants to sit close to the business. You will own forecasting, planning, metrics, cash visibility, and investor-ready reporting, while partnering closely with the Senior Accountant to make sure clean actuals flow into the right operating decisions. The goal is not to add bureaucracy. The goal is to give Garage a sharper financial model, better decision-making cadence, and a clearer view of what drives durable growth.

In this role, you will:

 - Build and own Garage’s operating model, including revenue, GMV, take rate, gross margin, burn, runway, headcount, and cash-flow forecasting.

 - Define and maintain the financial metrics that matter most for a marketplace business, turning transaction data into clear insights for founders, team leads, and investors.

 - Lead planning cycles across budgets, hiring plans, spend, and growth initiatives, helping the company understand the tradeoffs behind every major investment.

 - Monitor cash, runway, liquidity needs, and working capital dynamics across corporate operations and marketplace activity.

 - Build monthly and quarterly reporting packages that explain what is happening in the business, why it matters, and what decisions should follow.

 - Prepare investor materials, board updates, diligence support, and financing analyses as Garage grows.

 - Partner with the Accounting Lead on close outputs, transaction flows, funds movement, and reconciled actuals so planning and reporting stay grounded in reliable data.

 - Identify opportunities to improve margin, capital efficiency, vendor spend, and operating leverage without slowing the team down.

 - Build scalable finance systems, dashboards, metric definitions, and planning processes that can support Garage at its next stage and beyond.

What we’re looking for

 - Thinks like an owner and wants to be in the room for the company’s most important financial and strategic decisions.

 - Enjoys building models that become operating tools, not static spreadsheets that only finance understands.

 - Gets energized by the complexity of a marketplace business, including transaction volume, money movement, financing, freight, margin, and customer behavior.

 - Can move quickly with imperfect information, make thoughtful assumptions, and clearly explain what would change the recommendation.

 - Brings a high bar for analytical rigor, but knows when a fast, useful answer is better than a perfect answer that arrives too late.

 - Communicates clearly with founders and functional leaders, translating messy data into practical decisions.

 - Wants to build the foundation for a finance function that can scale.

Required

 - 5+ years experience in strategic finance, FP&A, finance and strategy, corporate finance, investment banking, private equity, consulting, business opera

source_jobs.title: Founding Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About us

Garage powers the exchange of critical assets across America’s essential industries. We started with fire departments, where purchasing mission-critical vehicles and equipment used to take months of paperwork, phone calls, and delays. Our agentic platform automates the entire transaction: discovery, payments, freight, paperwork, warranty, and financing. What once took months now takes minutes.

In under a year, we’ve expanded to cities and towns in all 50 states and grown revenue more than 10x. Based in New York, we’ve raised $18M from Y Combinator, Initialized Capital, Infinity Ventures, FJ Labs, and other leading investors. We’re moving fast, growing quickly, and just getting started.



About the role

Good design is a core feature of our platform. When people are looking to buy and sell assets that are valued as much as an actual house, it’s important the product experience is clean, smooth, and intuitive. Since the beginning, we’ve been very intentional about maintaining a high bar for product design.

We love going into the details, talking to our customers, and building the pixels nobody asked for but always wanted. You’ll join a small team working directly with the Co-founder and CTO.

 - Build and ship polished, production-quality UI across our core products (including a mobile app).

 - Craft UI details including layouts, typography, responsiveness, motion, and micro-interactions using Tailwind CSS.

 - Contribute to shared UI foundations including components, patterns, and design tokens.

 - Work across the stack using React, Next.js, and TypeScript to deliver fast, reliable interfaces.

 - Optimize for performance, accessibility, and maintainability without compromising on design.

 - Raise the bar for frontend craft through code reviews, critiques, and shared standards.



What we’re looking for

 - Strong eye for design and interaction with taste you can both defend and implement.

 - Comfort working across design, product, and engineering to ship work.

 - You think in systems, not tickets.

 - You get excited by operating in a high-performance startup environment.

 - You would love to be customer-facing.

 - You play for the front of the jersey, having strong opinions, a willingness to question the status quo, and a drive to become a real expert in whatever space you work in. All while being a great team communicator.



Required

 - Strong experience with React, Next.js, Tailwind, and TypeScript in production environments.

 - Exceptional CSS skills with a proven ability to build pixel-perfect, resilient user interfaces.

 - Portfolio that demonstrates high-craft frontend or design engineering work.



Nice to have

 - Startup experience as a technical co-founder or early engineer at a Series A/B/C startup.

 - 4+ years of experience.



Our values

 - Growth oriented. We invest in people who grow as the company grows. We all should be constantly learning.

 - Humility. Everyone has a lot to give and a lot to learn. We believe in creating an environment where the best ideas win and acknowledging when we are wrong.

 - Customer focused. We have a genuine love for our customers. Customer success is our success.

 - Hack to success. We move fast and take big swings. We always aim for a solution that addresses 80% of the problem in 20% of the time. We make informed bets, launch quickly, and iterate.



Why Garage?

 - Our product is something people use everyday to buy the equipment local communities everywhere depend on.

 - We’ve grown revenue over 10x in the last year and are well capitalized with $18M in funding raised total.

 - We have a talent-dense, high-performing team from Ramp, Twitter, Goldman Sachs, Bilt, Wise, Unify, Honey, PayPal, and more.

 - You’ll be one of our first 35 employees, operating as a foundational member of the team, partnering alongside the company’s founders.



What we offer

 - Competitive salary and stock options

 - Health, dental and vision insurance (100% covered)

source_jobs.title: Growth Marketing Lead, Lifecycle
source_jobs.description:
About us

Garage powers the exchange of critical assets across America’s essential industries. We started with fire departments, where purchasing mission-critical vehicles and equipment used to take months of paperwork, phone calls, and delays. Our agentic platform automates the entire transaction: discovery, payments, freight, paperwork, warranty, and financing. What once took months now takes minutes.

In under a year, we’ve expanded to cities and towns in all 50 states and grown revenue more than 10x. Based in New York, we’ve raised $18M from Y Combinator, Initialized Capital, Infinity Ventures, FJ Labs, and other leading investors. We’re moving fast, growing quickly, and just getting started.

About the role

We’re looking for a strong marketing generalist who spikes in the channels we own — email, SMS, and push — to be a key driver of growth and retention. You'll architect distinct journeys for buyers and sellers, finding ways to convert, re-engage, and streamline the buying and selling experience for our customers. This is a versatile role on a lean team, and you'll need to intimately understand the customer journey, marketing mix, and broader business goals.

You'll build this function from close to zero, with a strong creative team to pull from and a talented product team to support your programs.

 - Build and scale our owned channels. Architect and execute programs for both sides of the marketplace across email, SMS, and push. Your goal is to drive customer success and revenue growth through our most direct channels.

 - Turn users into customers. Help new users find success on the platform, quickly. Turn prospective buyers and sellers into transacting customers by understanding their needs and creating touch points that move them forward.

 - Turn customers into advocates. We have a lot of happy customers and want to amplify their voices. Build referral, affiliate, and advocacy programs that turn successful customers into repeat users and growth drivers.

 - Retain and re-engage. Drive repeat transactions and customer engagement post-sale. Map user journeys and identify opportunities and triggers to re-engage.

 - Balance supply and demand. Use lifecycle programs to improve liquidity, engagement, and transaction volume across both sides of the marketplace.

What we’re looking for

 - You love the energy of a high-growth environment where every day looks a little different

 - You’re able to go deep on lifecycle marketing but can think and work like a generalist

 - You have good judgment, high integrity, and a no-task-too-small mindset

Required

 - Bachelor’s degree from an accredited university

 - 4+ years of experience owning lifecycle marketing in a high-growth company

 - Have built and scaled lifecycle programs hands-on

 - Are T-shaped: deep in lifecycle and versatile across growth and marketing — comfortable wearing multiple hats to drive results.

 - Fluent with Customer.io http://Customer.io or similar platforms and comfortable in the data behind it

 - Can co-design systems with engineering and spec out the right events/triggers to feed the system

 - Comfortable testing and iterating quickly with analytical rigor

 - Thrive with high autonomy in an early, ambiguous environment

Nice to have

 - Experience scaling marketplace businesses

Our values

 - Growth oriented. We invest in people who grow as the company grows. We all should be constantly learning.

 - Humility. Everyone has a lot to give and a lot to learn. We believe in creating an environment where the best ideas win and acknowledging when we are wrong.

 - Customer focused. We have a genuine love for our customers. Customer success is our success.

 - Hack to success. We move fast and take big swings. We always aim for a solution that addresses 80% of the problem in 20% of the time. We make informed bets, launch quickly, and iterate.

Why Garage?

 - Our product is something people use everyday to buy the equipment local communities everywher

source_jobs.title: Growth Marketing Lead, Performance
source_jobs.description:
About us

Garage powers the exchange of critical assets across America’s essential industries. We started with fire departments, where purchasing mission-critical vehicles and equipment used to take months of paperwork, phone calls, and delays. Our agentic platform automates the entire transaction: discovery, payments, freight, paperwork, warranty, and financing. What once took months now takes minutes.

In under a year, we’ve expanded to cities and towns in all 50 states and grown revenue more than 10x. Based in New York, we’ve raised $18M from Y Combinator, Initialized Capital, Infinity Ventures, FJ Labs, and other leading investors. We’re moving fast, growing quickly, and just getting started.

About the role

We're looking for a Growth Marketing Lead, Performance to own paid acquisition and drive broader growth in the business. As a member of a lean and hands-on team, you'll manage our entire paid portfolio across paid social, search, and emerging channels, and work closely with the Head of Marketing to continue building and scaling Garage’s growth machine.

The ideal candidate is someone who balances analytical rigor with the understanding that the biggest growth drivers happen outside the ad account.

 - Own Garage’s paid acquisition strategy and execution across paid social, paid search, and emerging channels, with accountability for pipeline, revenue growth, and efficiency.

 - Scale acquisition efficiently by balancing growth, CAC, and overall marketplace health. Understand what changes at scale and make disciplined decisions around where to invest.

 - Test and learn, constantly. You will develop hypotheses, test, and iterate fast. You’ll orchestrate landing page and conversion rate tests, analyze creative performance, and think creatively about new ways to acquire customers efficiently.

 - Measure rigorously, act intuitively. You read between the lines and see the bigger picture in the numbers. You understand incrementality, multi-touch attribution, and test design, yet are comfortable navigating when data is incomplete or ambiguous.

 - Work cross-functionally to identify and unlock growth opportunities beyond paid acquisition, partnering closely with Sales, Product, Operations, and Marketing.

What we’re looking for

 - You love the energy of a high-growth environment where every day looks a little different

 - You like to go deep on performance marketing but can think and work like a generalist

 - You have good judgment, high integrity, and a no-task-too-small mindset

Required

 - Bachelor’s degree from an accredited university

 - 4+ years owning meaningful paid acquisition budgets in a high-growth startup or technology company

 - Demonstrated success scaling paid social and search programs while maintaining efficiency

 - Are T-Shaped: deep and hands-on in paid, and genuinely versatile across the growth mix — you've owned or can credibly own adjacent levers (CRO, partnerships, web experiments)

 - Are rigorous about measurement and can tell real incremental growth from vanity metrics

 - Have strong creative instincts and can direct high-velocity creative testing by briefing a creative team well

 - Thrive as a player-coach on a small team — in the accounts yourself, leading a teammate, holding vendors accountable, etc.

Nice to have

 - Experience scaling marketplace businesses

 - Have built and scaled accounts from scratch

 - Customer focused. We have a genuine love for our customers. Customer success is our success.

source_jobs.title: Senior Accountant
source_jobs.description:
About us

Garage powers the exchange of critical assets across America’s essential industries. We started with fire departments, where purchasing mission-critical vehicles and equipment used to take months of paperwork, phone calls, and delays. Our agentic platform automates the entire transaction: discovery, payments, freight, paperwork, warranty, and financing. What once took months now takes minutes.

In under a year, we’ve expanded to cities and towns in all 50 states and grown revenue more than 10x. Based in New York, we’ve raised $18M from Y Combinator, Initialized Capital, Infinity Ventures, FJ Labs, and other leading investors. We’re moving fast, growing quickly, and just getting started.

About the role

As Accounting Lead, you will own the accounting foundation for Garage as we scale a transaction-heavy marketplace across essential industries. Reporting directly to the CEO, you’ll partner closely with the Finance Lead to keep the books accurate, the close reliable, and the accounting operations ready for the next stage of growth.

In this role, you will

 - Own Garage’s books in QuickBooks Online, including accurate financial statements, account reconciliations, and month-end close.

 - Lead GAAP accrual accounting across AP, AR, payroll coordination, vendor payments, expenses, sales commissions, and related workflows.

 - Reconcile marketplace transactions, payments, settlements, and funds movement across internal systems and external partners.

 - Build and improve accounting processes, close calendars, review routines, documentation, and controls that can scale with the business.

 - Partner with the Finance Lead to provide clean, reliable accounting inputs for planning, reporting, and business decision-making.

 - Identify and resolve accounting operations issues quickly, bringing structure and clarity to complex or ambiguous workflows.

 - Help Garage prepare for future audits, diligence requests, systems upgrades, and a more mature accounting function as the company grows.

What we're looking for

 - Loves being hands-on with the details while also building systems that make the whole company more reliable.

 - Feels excited by the accounting complexity of a fast-growing marketplace, including high transaction volume, money movement, and operational edge cases.

 - Enjoys creating order from ambiguity and can move quickly without sacrificing accuracy or judgment.

 - Gets motivated by owning the accounting function at an early-stage company where the foundation you build will matter for years.

 - Has a high bar for precision, follow-through, and financial integrity, especially when money movement and customer trust are involved.

 - Works well with founders and cross-functional teams, translating accounting needs into clear actions and practical processes.

 - Brings an operator mindset from a lean finance or accounting team and prefers solving real business problems over adding unnecessary complexity.

Required

 - 3+ years of accounting experience, ideally including ownership of accounting operations in a lean or high-growth environment.

 - Bachelor’s degree in accounting, finance, or a related field.

 - Strong experience with QuickBooks Online and proficiency in Microsoft Excel.

 - Strong knowledge of GAAP accrual accounting, including AP, AR, month-end close, reconciliations, and financial statement management.

 - Excellent reconciliation skills across multiple bank accounts, payment flows, systems, and vendors.

 - Ability to build, document, and improve accounting processes rather than only execute existing ones.

 - Strong organizational judgment and follow-through, with the ability to manage multiple accounting workstreams at once.

 - Comfort operating in ambiguity and taking ownership in a fast-moving, early-stage environment.

Nice to have

 - CPA or CPA-track.

 - Experience in marketplaces, fintech, payments, lending, or other transaction-heavy businesses.

 - Experience at an early-stage star

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Associate
source_jobs.description:
About us

Garage powers the exchange of critical assets across America’s essential industries. We started with fire departments, where purchasing mission-critical vehicles and equipment used to take months of paperwork, phone calls, and delays. Our agentic platform automates the entire transaction: discovery, payments, freight, paperwork, warranty, and financing. What once took months now takes minutes.

In under a year, we’ve expanded to cities and towns in all 50 states and grown revenue more than 10x. Based in New York, we’ve raised $18M from Y Combinator, Initialized Capital, Infinity Ventures, FJ Labs, and other leading investors. We’re moving fast, growing quickly, and just getting started.

About the role

We’re looking for a Customer Success Associate to deliver a fast, thoughtful support experience for the buyers and sellers who use Garage. You’ll be the first point of contact for customer questions across phone, email, chat, and text, helping customers navigate transaction questions, resolve issues, and get clear answers quickly.

As an early member of our customer success function, you’ll also help build the systems that let Garage support more customers without losing quality: Help Center content, internal documentation, escalation workflows, support tooling, and customer feedback loops. This is a hands-on role for someone who loves helping customers directly and wants to help shape how Garage delivers customer experience at scale.

In this role, you will:

 - Own day-to-day customer support across phone, email, chat, and text for buyers and sellers.

 - Triage, investigate, and resolve customer issues from intake through follow-up, escalating to Ops, Product, Finance, or Go-to-Market when needed.

 - Support purchase management workflows by helping customers understand next steps, coordinating internal handoffs, and keeping transactions moving.

 - Build and maintain Help Center articles, knowledge-base content, macros, and customer-facing documentation for a non-technical audience.

 - Improve support workflows with tooling, AI, and automation to reduce repetitive work and create faster, clearer customer responses.

 - Track recurring questions, pain points, and customer feedback, then share actionable insights with internal teams.

 - Help define the standards, workflows, and documentation habits that will shape customer success at Garage as we grow.

What we’re looking for

 - You care deeply about customers and take pride in making complex situations feel clear and manageable.

 - You communicate with warmth, precision, and urgency across phone, email, chat, and text.

 - You stay organized when several customer issues are moving at once and follow through until each issue is resolved.

 - You know when to solve independently, when to escalate, and how to keep customers informed along the way.

 - You look for repeatable fixes, not just one-off answers, and enjoy turning customer questions into better systems and documentation.

 - You bring sound judgment, integrity, and a no-task-too-small mindset to work that directly affects customer trust.

 - You’re excited to help build an early customer success function in a fast-moving startup environment.

Required

 - 2+ years of experience in customer support, customer success, customer experience, or another customer-facing role

 - Experience supporting customers across multiple channels, including phone, email, chat, and text

 - Strong verbal and written communication skills

 - Comfort learning new customer support tools, internal systems, and operational processes quickly.

 - Good judgment when triaging issues, escalating appropriately, and communicating with customers.

Nice to have

 - Experience using modern customer support platforms such as Intercom, Front, Pylon, or similar tools.

 - Experience working at a startup or high-growth technology company.

 - Experience with marketplaces, logistics, payments, procurement, equipment, or other operationally c

source_jobs.title: Event Marketing Lead
source_jobs.description:
About us

Garage powers the exchange of critical assets across America’s essential industries. We started with fire departments, where purchasing mission-critical vehicles and equipment used to take months of paperwork, phone calls, and delays. Our agentic platform automates the entire transaction: discovery, payments, freight, paperwork, warranty, and financing. What once took months now takes minutes.

In under a year, we’ve expanded to cities and towns in all 50 states and grown revenue more than 10x. Based in New York, we’ve raised $18M from Y Combinator, Initialized Capital, Infinity Ventures, FJ Labs, and other leading investors. We’re moving fast, growing quickly, and just getting started.

About the role

Garage is hiring an Event Marketing Lead to build the events engine that turns high-intent industry moments into a qualified pipeline. As Garage scales its go-to-market motion, events are becoming a critical way for us to get in front of the right audience, deepen customer relationships, and create more high-quality sales conversations.

This is a hands-on individual contributor role for someone who can own the full event motion: choosing the right conferences and field opportunities, building the plan with Sales, managing the logistics, driving attendance, and making sure every event has a measurable follow-up strategy.

You will partner closely with Sales, Marketing, and company leadership to identify the rooms Garage should be in, create thoughtful experiences for buyers and customers, and translate event activity into conversations, opportunities, and revenue.

In this role you will

 - Own Garage’s pipeline-focused event calendar across conferences, trade shows, field events, dinners, and other in-person customer or prospect experiences.

 - Manage event execution end to end, including timelines, budgets, vendors, venues, booth or sponsorship deliverables, shipping, staffing, collateral, and day-of logistics.

 - Build repeatable playbooks for event planning, sales coordination, lead capture, follow-up, and post-event reporting.

 - Create strong attendee experiences that make Garage clear, credible, and memorable to buyers in essential industries.

 - Partner with Sales to define event goals, target accounts, attendee strategy, pre-event outreach, on-site coverage, and post-event follow-up.

 - Evaluate which events Garage should sponsor, attend, host, or skip based on audience fit, pipeline potential, cost, and strategic value.

 - Work with Marketing and Sales to turn event moments into campaigns, customer touchpoints, executive meetings, and pipeline-generating follow-up.

 - Track event performance, report on outcomes, and use learnings to improve Garage’s event strategy over time.

What we’re looking for

 - You are a practical, high-ownership event marketer who can create structure from zero and connect event work directly to business outcomes.

 - You are comfortable working closely with Sales, managing multiple moving pieces at once, and making clear recommendations about where Garage should spend time and money.

 - You bring strong judgment, crisp execution, and a bias toward turning every event into real customer conversations.

Required

 - 2+ years of experience planning and executing events such as conferences, trade shows, field events, customer dinners, or executive experiences.

 - Strong project management skills, including the ability to manage timelines, vendors, budgets, stakeholders, and logistics without dropping details.

 - Clear written and verbal communication, especially when coordinating across internal teams and external partners.

 - Ability to build lightweight processes, make tradeoffs quickly, and execute in an environment where the event program is still being built.

 - Ability to travel for events as needed, approximately 2-5 days a month

Nice to have

 - Experience building an event marketing program or repeatable event playbooks from an early stage.

 - Experience marketin

source_jobs.title: Inside Sales Representative
source_jobs.description:
About us

Garage powers the exchange of critical assets across America’s essential industries. We started with fire departments, where purchasing mission-critical vehicles and equipment used to take months of paperwork, phone calls, and delays. Our agentic platform automates the entire transaction: discovery, payments, freight, paperwork, warranty, and financing. What once took months now takes minutes.

In under a year, we’ve expanded to cities and towns in all 50 states and grown revenue more than 10x. Based in New York, we’ve raised $18M from Y Combinator, Initialized Capital, Infinity Ventures, FJ Labs, and other leading investors. We’re moving fast, growing quickly, and just getting started.

About the role

Garage’s Go-to-Market team is on a mission to build a broader more powerful marketplace by expanding categories and accelerating seller and buyer growth.

The Inside Sales Representative role is ideal for someone early in their career who wants to develop into a top-performing Account Executive. You’ll work directly with customers, own deals from first conversation through close, and partner closely with experienced sales leaders, Founders, product, and operations teams.

 - Take end-to-end ownership of the inside sales cycle from discovery to close

 - Engage directly with customers to understand their needs and deliver impact

 - Generate new opportunities, follow up on leads, and consistently move deals forward

 - Surface insights from customer conversations and work cross-functionally with Product, Ops, and company Founders to help shape our product and strategy roadmap.

What we’re looking for

 - You’re competitive and love to win

 - You move fast, have an ownership mentality, and an entrepreneurial mindset

 - You are a relationship builder who deeply enjoys getting to know customers and being the conduit between the business and users

 - You’re resilient and tenacious who's always pushing to uncover and win new business opportunities

 - You have strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to articulate value propositions in an effective manner

 - You enjoy storytelling and compelling narratives that clearly communicate our value

 - You are excited to champion a product that creates real-world impact for both users and the communities they serve

Required

 - You have 1-2 years of experience in a client-facing role with an ability to work quickly and iteratively, and are looking to build a foundation in sales and client management.
   
   We especially value candidates coming from consulting, banking, business development or closing roles at early stage startups.

Our values

 - Growth oriented. We invest in people who grow as the company grows. We all should be constantly learning.

 - Humility. Everyone has a lot to give and a lot to learn. We believe in creating an environment where the best ideas win and acknowledging when we are wrong.

 - Customer focused. We have a genuine love for our customers. Customer success is our success.

 - Hack to success. We move fast and take big swings. We always aim for a solution that addresses 80% of the problem in 20% of the time. We make informed bets, launch quickly, and iterate.

Why Garage?

 - Our product is something people use everyday to buy the equipment local communities everywhere depend on.

 - We’ve grown revenue over 10x in the last year and are well capitalized with $18M in funding raised total.

 - We have a talent-dense, high-performing team from Ramp, Twitter, Goldman Sachs, Bilt, Wise, Unify, Honey, PayPal, and more.

 - You’ll be one of our first 35 employees, operating as a foundational member of the team, partnering alongside the company’s founders.

What we offer

 - Competitive salary and stock options

 - Health, dental and vision insurance (100% covered)

 - Unlimited paid time off every year (in addition to company observed holidays)

 - $100 monthly wellness stipend

 - 401(k) with employer match

 - Bi-annual team of

source_jobs.title: Organic Growth & Partnerships Lead
source_jobs.description:
About us

Garage powers the exchange of critical assets across America’s essential industries. We started with fire departments, where purchasing mission-critical vehicles and equipment used to take months of paperwork, phone calls, and delays. Our agentic platform automates the entire transaction: discovery, payments, freight, paperwork, warranty, and financing. What once took months now takes minutes.

In under a year, we’ve expanded to cities and towns in all 50 states and grown revenue more than 10x. Based in New York, we’ve raised $18M from Y Combinator, Initialized Capital, Infinity Ventures, FJ Labs, and other leading investors. We’re moving fast, growing quickly, and just getting started.



About the role

We're looking for a strong marketing generalist to be a key driver of organic growth. You'll find creative and cost-efficient ways to acquire customers, grow our audience, and build our brand across a variety of surfaces, including but not limited to organic social, partnerships, press, and SEO/AEO. This is a versatile role on a lean team, and you'll need a deep understanding of what actually drives impact and a bias to action and experimentation.

This is a role for a scrappy, growth-oriented marketer who is excited by building organic flywheels that scale with the business. You'll work closely with the Head of Marketing to build these functions from close to zero and develop systems to scale them.

 - Build and scale our organic and earned channels. Own growth across organic social, press, and content. Your goal is to grow our audience and drive acquisition through channels that compound over time.

 - Build partnerships that drive growth. Develop and manage partnerships across creators, industry associations, and platforms that put us in front of new buyers and sellers and open up net-new marketing opportunities.

 - Turn customers into advocates. We have a lot of happy customers and want to amplify their voices. Build referral, affiliate, and advocacy programs that turn successful customers into repeat users and growth drivers.

 - Win search and answer engines. Build our presence across SEO and AI answer engines (AEO) so we show up when prospective customers — and the models they ask — are looking.

 - Experiment with new channels. Test into new channels and special projects, and build repeatable systems to acquire buyers and sellers. Drop what doesn't work and double down on what does.

 - Take leveraged swings. Collaborate across marketing, sales, and engineering to find plays that drive outsized, compounding growth.



What we’re looking for

 - You love the energy of a high-growth environment where every day looks a little different

 - You're fast-moving, resourceful, and have a strong bias to action — down to do what it takes to move the business forward.

 - You can go deep on organic and social growth but think and work like a generalist

 - You have good judgment, high integrity, and a no-task-too-small mindset



Required

 - Bachelor's degree from an accredited university

 - 4+ years of experience owning organic, social, or growth marketing in a high-growth company

 - Have built and scaled organic growth programs hands-on — with proof they drove real growth

 - Are T-shaped: deep in some aspects of the role, and comfortable wearing multiple hats to drive results

 - Know what makes great organic content that grows both followings and businesses

 - Comfortable building repeatable systems and testing and iterating quickly

 - Thrive with high autonomy in an early, ambiguous environment



Nice to have

 - Experience scaling marketplace businesses

 - Experience growing social channels organically (YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, etc)

 - A track record of community-led growth

 - Existing relationships with creators or press



Our values

 - Growth oriented. We invest in people who grow as the company grows. We all should be constantly learning.

 - Humility. Everyone has a lot to give and a lot to l

notes: High-growth startup with strong engineering focus, expanding rapidly across the US.
